steps.title: Interpret lower creep rate
steps.content: A lower minimum creep rate means the nitrogen-treated SiC fiber deforms more slowly under constant stress than the two polymer-derived fibers. Slower deformation suggests better resistance over time.

steps.title: Infer longer stress resistance
steps.content: Choice A overstates the finding as substantially inhibiting creep. Choice B says reducing creep resistance would prolong machinery life, which reverses the logic. Choice C says polymer-derived fibers are better, opposite of the evidence. Nitrogen-treated composites may withstand mechanical stress longer. The answer is D.
